Fin Whale Modeling Template

Load Packages and Data

# Load in the libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(sdmTMB)
library(ggplot2)
library(janitor)
library(sp)
library(readr)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(Metrics)
library(naniar)
library(tibble)
library(rsample)
library(caret)
library(purrr)

# Read in the data
#cal_data <- read.csv("/Users/valeriedelafuente/Documents/Capstone/capstone-scripps/data/Merged_CalCOFI/STD_CalCOFI_final_merged_data_2012-2023.csv")

cal_data <- read.csv("/Users/josh/Documents/GitHub/capstone-scripps/data/Merged_CalCOFI/STD_CalCOFI_final_merged_data_2012-2023.csv")

Data Cleaning

cal_data$season <- as.factor(cal_data$season)

# Create a new `seasonal_category` column
cal_data <- cal_data %>% 
  mutate(seasonal_category = case_when(
    month %in% c(12, 1, 2)  ~ "Winter",
    month %in% c(3, 4, 5)   ~ "Spring",
    month %in% c(6, 7, 8)   ~ "Summer",
    month %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Fall"
  ))

# Removing the first two columns
cal_data <- cal_data[, -c(1, 2)]

# Create fin whale response column
cal_data <- cal_data %>%
  mutate(fin_resp = x20hz_scaled + x40hz_scaled)

Exploratory Data Analysis

Correlation Plot

# Plotting Correlation
# Define expected columns
cols_to_use <- c("est_depth", 
                 "temperature_depth_105", "temperature_depth_280", "temperature_depth_55", 
                 "salinity_depth_105", "salinity_depth_280", "salinity_depth_55", 
                 "meridional_velocity_depth_105", "meridional_velocity_depth_280", "meridional_velocity_depth_55", 
                 "zonal_velocity_depth_105", "zonal_velocity_depth_280", "zonal_velocity_depth_55", 
                 "vertical_velocity_depth_105", "vertical_velocity_depth_280", "vertical_velocity_55")

# Keep only existing columns
existing_cols <- intersect(cols_to_use, colnames(cal_data))
cor_data <- cal_data[, existing_cols, drop = FALSE]

# Compute correlation matrix (use complete.obs to skip missing data)
cor_matrix <- cor(cor_data, use = "complete.obs")

# Correlation plot
ggcorrplot(cor_matrix, 
           lab = TRUE, 
           hc.order = TRUE, 
           lab_size =2,
           tl.cex = 5,
           type = "lower", 
           title = "Correlation Plot")

Missing Data

# Count how many missing values there are
sum(is.na(cal_data))
## [1] 62598
# Show the dimensions of the dataframe
dim(cal_data)
## [1] 4250   77
# Visualize missing data
missing_data_plot <- naniar::vis_miss(cal_data)
print(missing_data_plot)

# Count how many values are present in the response
sum(!is.na(cal_data$fin_resp))
## [1] 1079
# Check across 
gg_miss_fct(cal_data, fct = seasonal_category)

# Changing dataset to only have present response data
cal_data <- cal_data %>% 
  filter(!is.na(fin_resp))

# Visualize missing data with all responses present
missing_data_resp_plot <- naniar::vis_miss(cal_data)
print(missing_data_resp_plot)

Autocorrelation Detection

cal_data_resp_pres <- cal_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(fin_resp))

acf(cal_data_resp_pres$fin_resp)

pacf(cal_data_resp_pres$fin_resp)

Splitting the Data

# Split data into training and testing sets with most recent year as our testing set
#since we have problems with 2023, remove that year for now

bad_years <- c(2015, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023)
cal_data <- cal_data %>% filter(!year %in% bad_years)

#cal_data <- cal_data %>% filter(year != 2022)


set.seed(123)
last_year <- max(cal_data$year)
train_data <- cal_data %>% filter(year != last_year)
test_data <- cal_data %>% filter(year == last_year)

Modeling

Forward Stepwise Selection

# Define the initial model with only the intercept
current_model <- lm(fin_resp ~ 1, data = train_data)

# Define the full model with all predictors
full_model <- lm(fin_resp ~ temperature_depth_105_std + temperature_depth_55_std + temperature_depth_280_std + salinity_depth_55_std + salinity_depth_105_std + magnitude_depth_55_std + magnitude_depth_105_std + theta_depth_55_std + theta_depth_105_std, 
data = train_data)

# Perform forward stepwise selection using the step() function
best_stepwise_model <- step(current_model,
                            scope = list(lower = current_model, upper = full_model),
                            direction = "forward")
## Start:  AIC=3106.92
## fin_resp ~ 1
## 
##                             Df Sum of Sq    RSS    AIC
## + salinity_depth_105_std     1   11498.1 551744 3100.0
## + salinity_depth_55_std      1   11324.7 551917 3100.1
## + magnitude_depth_55_std     1    9758.2 553484 3101.4
## + magnitude_depth_105_std    1    7374.4 555868 3103.2
## + temperature_depth_55_std   1    5319.5 557923 3104.8
## + temperature_depth_105_std  1    3853.3 559389 3105.9
## <none>                                   563242 3106.9
## + theta_depth_55_std         1    1871.7 561370 3107.5
## + theta_depth_105_std        1    1454.9 561787 3107.8
## + temperature_depth_280_std  1    1331.7 561910 3107.9
## 
## Step:  AIC=3099.99
## fin_resp ~ salinity_depth_105_std
## 
##                             Df Sum of Sq    RSS    AIC
## + temperature_depth_55_std   1    6510.7 545233 3096.8
## + temperature_depth_105_std  1    4421.1 547323 3098.5
## + magnitude_depth_55_std     1    4092.9 547651 3098.8
## + magnitude_depth_105_std    1    2866.7 548877 3099.7
## <none>                                   551744 3100.0
## + theta_depth_55_std         1    2350.8 549393 3100.2
## + theta_depth_105_std        1    2104.6 549639 3100.3
## + temperature_depth_280_std  1     793.8 550950 3101.4
## + salinity_depth_55_std      1     236.7 551507 3101.8
## 
## Step:  AIC=3096.85
## fin_resp ~ salinity_depth_105_std + temperature_depth_55_std
## 
##                             Df Sum of Sq    RSS    AIC
## + temperature_depth_280_std  1   11178.2 534055 3089.9
## + temperature_depth_105_std  1    8769.3 536464 3091.8
## + magnitude_depth_55_std     1    3645.2 541588 3095.9
## <none>                                   545233 3096.8
## + theta_depth_55_std         1    2486.0 542747 3096.9
## + theta_depth_105_std        1    2127.0 543106 3097.2
## + magnitude_depth_105_std    1    1806.7 543427 3097.4
## + salinity_depth_55_std      1      67.0 545166 3098.8
## 
## Step:  AIC=3089.88
## fin_resp ~ salinity_depth_105_std + temperature_depth_55_std + 
##     temperature_depth_280_std
## 
##                             Df Sum of Sq    RSS    AIC
## + theta_depth_55_std         1    2870.4 531185 3089.6
## + magnitude_depth_55_std     1    2790.5 531265 3089.6
## <none>                                   534055 3089.9
## + salinity_depth_55_std      1    2056.4 531999 3090.2
## + theta_depth_105_std        1    1864.1 532191 3090.4
## + magnitude_depth_105_std    1    1341.4 532714 3090.8
## + temperature_depth_105_std  1      79.2 533976 3091.8
## 
## Step:  AIC=3089.55
## fin_resp ~ salinity_depth_105_std + temperature_depth_55_std + 
##     temperature_depth_280_std + theta_depth_55_std
## 
##                             Df Sum of Sq    RSS    AIC
## <none>                                   531185 3089.6
## + magnitude_depth_55_std     1   2346.54 528838 3089.6
## + salinity_depth_55_std      1   1932.76 529252 3090.0
## + magnitude_depth_105_std    1   1116.96 530068 3090.6
## + temperature_depth_105_std  1     72.06 531113 3091.5
## + theta_depth_105_std        1     48.94 531136 3091.5

Tweedie Model

# TWEEDIE

# Create mesh using training data
mesh_train <- make_mesh(train_data,
                        xy_cols = c("X", "Y"),
                        cutoff = 10)
plot(mesh_train)

train_data$season <- droplevels(train_data$season)


# Fit spatiotemporal model
fit_spatiotemporal <- sdmTMB(
  fin_resp ~ est_depth_std + temperature_depth_105_std + temperature_depth_55_std + temperature_depth_280_std + salinity_depth_55_std + salinity_depth_105_std + magnitude_depth_55_std + magnitude_depth_105_std + theta_depth_55_std + theta_depth_105_std, 
  data = train_data, 
  mesh = mesh_train,
  time = "year",
  family = tweedie(link = "log"), 
  spatial = "off", 
  spatiotemporal = "iid",
  extra_time = unique(test_data$season)
)

# Extract coefficients and sanity check
tidy(fit_spatiotemporal, conf.int = TRUE)
## # A tibble: 11 × 5
##    term                       estimate std.error conf.low conf.high
##    <chr>                         <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>
##  1 (Intercept)                 0.913       0.244    0.434     1.39 
##  2 est_depth_std              -0.740       0.223   -1.18     -0.303
##  3 temperature_depth_105_std  -0.00548     2.40    -4.72      4.71 
##  4 temperature_depth_55_std   -2.88        1.86    -6.53      0.777
##  5 temperature_depth_280_std   2.60        0.743    1.14      4.05 
##  6 salinity_depth_55_std      11.6         5.71     0.447    22.8  
##  7 salinity_depth_105_std    -11.4         5.72   -22.6      -0.208
##  8 magnitude_depth_55_std     -0.395       0.415   -1.21      0.418
##  9 magnitude_depth_105_std     0.268       0.413   -0.541     1.08 
## 10 theta_depth_55_std         -0.0838      0.263   -0.599     0.431
## 11 theta_depth_105_std        -0.149       0.254   -0.648     0.349
sanity(fit_spatiotemporal)
## ✔ Non-linear minimizer suggests successful convergence
## ✔ Hessian matrix is positive definite
## ✔ No extreme or very small eigenvalues detected
## ✔ No gradients with respect to fixed effects are >= 0.001
## ✔ No fixed-effect standard errors are NA
## ✔ No standard errors look unreasonably large
## ✔ No sigma parameters are < 0.01
## ✔ No sigma parameters are > 100
## ✔ Range parameter doesn't look unreasonably large
# Predictions on training and test data
training_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = train_data)
training_pred$observed <- train_data$fin_resp

#training_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = train_data, type = "response")
#training_pred$observed <- train_data$fin_resp[as.numeric(rownames(training_pred))]


test_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = test_data)
test_pred$observed <- test_data$fin_resp

#test_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = test_data, type = "response", #extra_time = setdiff(unique(test_data$season), unique(train_data$season)))

test_pred$observed <- test_data$fin_resp[as.numeric(rownames(test_pred))]
# Plotting the predictions
p0 <- ggplot(training_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Train") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

p1 <- ggplot(test_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Test") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p0, p1)

# Compute RMSE
rmse_train <- rmse(training_pred$observed, training_pred$est)
rmse_test <- rmse(test_pred$observed, test_pred$est)

# Compute Range
range_train <- range(train_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)
range_test <- range(test_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)

# Compute Standard Deviation
sd_train <- sd(train_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_test <- sd(test_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)

# Assemble the table
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("Training", "Test"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_train, rmse_test),
  Range_Min = c(range_train[1], range_test[1]),
  Range_Max = c(range_train[2], range_test[2]),
  Std_Dev = c(sd_train, sd_test)
)

# Print the summary table
print(summary_table)
##    Dataset     RMSE Range_Min Range_Max  Std_Dev
## 1 Training 36.76344         0  389.2269 36.10819
## 2     Test 30.02535         0  198.0852 29.16946

Delta-Gamma Model

# DELTA GAMMA

# Create mesh using training data
mesh_train <- make_mesh(train_data,
                        xy_cols = c("X", "Y"),
                        cutoff = 10)
plot(mesh_train)

# Fit spatiotemporal model
fit_spatiotemporal <- sdmTMB(
  fin_resp ~ est_depth_std + temperature_depth_105_std + temperature_depth_55_std + temperature_depth_280_std + salinity_depth_55_std + salinity_depth_105_std + magnitude_depth_55_std + magnitude_depth_105_std + theta_depth_55_std + theta_depth_105_std + larvae_count_std, 
  data = train_data, 
  mesh = mesh_train,
  time = "season",
  family = delta_gamma(link1 = "logit", link2 = "log"), 
  spatial = "off", 
  spatiotemporal = "iid",
  extra_time = unique(test_data$season)
)

# Extract coefficients and sanity check
tidy(fit_spatiotemporal, conf.int = TRUE)
sanity(fit_spatiotemporal)

# Predictions on training and test data
training_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = train_data, type = "response")
training_pred$observed <- train_data$fin_resp

test_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
test_pred$observed <- test_data$fin_resp

# Plotting the predictions
p0 <- ggplot(training_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Train") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

p1 <- ggplot(test_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Test") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p0, p1)

# Compute RMSE
rmse_train <- rmse(training_pred$observed, training_pred$est)
rmse_test <- rmse(test_pred$observed, test_pred$est)

# Compute Range
range_train <- range(train_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)
range_test <- range(test_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)

# Compute Standard Deviation
sd_train <- sd(train_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_test <- sd(test_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)

# Assemble the table
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("Training", "Test"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_train, rmse_test),
  Range_Min = c(range_train[1], range_test[1]),
  Range_Max = c(range_train[2], range_test[2]),
  Std_Dev = c(sd_train, sd_test)
)

# Print the summary table
print(summary_table)
